batchingRulesApplied: B2 and B4 each group 4 decisions sharing an identical semantic question across workouts, not merely sharing a file
B3, B5, B6, B7 each correspond to one pre-existing inseparable audit row/artifact, not artificially split
D5/D6 on the same artifact (EASY_STANDARD) correctly split across B2/B4 because they are different semantic questions
executionOrderNotes: B5 and B6 may run in parallel with each other and with B1. B2 must precede B3 (tier-scale consumer dependency). B4 may run in parallel with B2/B3 but is sequenced after to keep per-workout review coherent. B7 is sequenced last as the most evidence-starved, most likely product-owner-dependent decision.

Convene explicit Process A/Process B ownership conversation
Search for a Tier 1/2 experience-segmented peak-volume table; escalate to NEEDS_PRODUCT_DECISION if none found

Product owner decides: commission new research, approve explicit default, or remove from active closure

CANONICAL_CONFIRMED: direct or clearly applicable evidence from an approved canonical source (never inferred from a single fixture instance)
conflict analysis per docs/README.md section 1 precedence when sources disagree
no contradiction with Golden Fixture v3
no unsupported generalization from a single realized instance
EXPLICIT_PRODUCT_DEFAULT: documented evidence canonical sources do not uniquely determine the value (negative search documented)
explicit user/product-owner approval, dated and attributable
TECHNICAL_ONLY: proof the value does not encode a running-domain claim
proof it does not alter training behavior directly or indirectly
must never be used merely to remove a decision from the Production blocker count
fieldRemoval: proof the field is unused or semantically redundant
schema and compatibility impact analysis (schema files affected, published-artifact shape impact, test assertions on the field)

finalStatus: Resolution plan complete: 7 batches, recommended execution order established, no decision resolved.
